Title: Dutchman's Pipe
Description: An ultra-private 220-acre club in the heart of West Palm Beach built around a 7,287-yard Jack Nicklaus Signature Course with no tee times, a tour-level practice range, a racquet club, a spa, multiple dining venues, and 22 private villas.
DescriptionLong: An ultra-private members club set on 220 acres east of I-95 in the heart of West Palm Beach, built around a 7,287-yard Jack Nicklaus Signature Course played with no tee times — long, generously spaced fairways framed by native landscaping, including the flowering Dutchman's Pipe vine that gives the club its name. The practice experience runs to tour-stop standards: a driving range exceeding 300 yards stocked with every ball on the market, two acres devoted to the short game, Top 100 instruction, and an on-site custom club-building operation that can fit and assemble a club to a player's specs within minutes. Beyond golf, a cutting-edge racquet club brings together tennis, padel, and pickleball, alongside a fitness center, a day spa, personal training, and event spaces. Three dining venues — including a members-only grill, poolside al fresco dining, and a dining room with a balcony overlooking the range and the first hole — round out the experience, and a private collection of 22 villas sits steps from the club, connected to an adjacent Marriott Autograph Collection resort and spa on the same property.
Developer: Witkoff Group, Access Industries, PPG Development

Title: Eden Residences
Description: A boutique 10-story condominium in Ojus with just 32 residences, a rooftop sky deck with a resort pool and spa, and wellness and fitness amenities scaled to a small resident community — minutes from Aventura Mall, Brightline, and the coast.
DescriptionLong: A boutique new-construction condominium in the Ojus neighborhood just west of Aventura, Eden rises 10 stories (about 110 feet) with only 32 private residences — an intentionally intimate alternative to the area's largest towers. Homes start around 711 square feet and span one-bedroom-plus-den, two-, and three-bedroom layouts, with floor-to-ceiling glazing, culinary-grade kitchens, stone-and-water bathroom sanctuaries, and assigned parking. Amenities include a rooftop sky deck with a resort-style pool and spa, a fitness center, and a sauna, all programmed for a small resident population rather than a crowd. The building permits monthly short-term rentals with no long-term-only restriction, and includes community-minded touches such as a Shabbat elevator. Eden sits steps from a sister boutique tower, Lev, by the same team, and minutes from Aventura Mall, the Brightline station, area golf, and the Atlantic.

Title: Siari, a Ritz-Carlton Reserve
Description: A 91-key Ritz-Carlton Reserve anchoring the private Nauka community on Mexico's Riviera Nayarit — the brand's ninth Reserve worldwide and second in Mexico, set between Pacific coastline and old-growth jungle.
DescriptionLong: Siari, a Ritz-Carlton Reserve, is the resort heart of the private 920-acre Nauka community on the Riviera Nayarit, roughly 45 minutes north of Puerto Vallarta. As the ninth Ritz-Carlton Reserve in the world and the second in Mexico, the 91-key retreat sits among the same dramatic landscapes that define Nauka — Pacific coastline, volcanic cliffs, mangrove estuary, and old-growth jungle — with architecture and interiors by Bernardi + Peschard. Guests draw on the wider community's experiences, from the Tom Fazio championship golf course and beach club to the marina, wellness sanctuary, and adventure program, and beginning in 2026 a limited number of guests receive curated access to tee times at Nauka Golf Club — for now, one of the only doors into the community.

Title: Four Seasons Private Residences Coconut Grove
Description: Florida's first standalone Four Seasons–branded residence — a 20-story waterfront tower on Biscayne Bay with just 70 homes, curved walls of glass, a Roman-inspired spa, and full Four Seasons service, with no hotel attached.
DescriptionLong: Standing in the front row on Biscayne Bay in Coconut Grove, Four Seasons Private Residences Coconut Grove is the first standalone Four Seasons–branded residence in Florida — a purely residential tower with no hotel component, a rarity in the global Four Seasons portfolio. The 20-story building holds only 70 waterfront homes of generous proportions, with two- to four-bedroom layouts reaching nearly 4,000 square feet, 11-foot ceilings, expansive curved balconies, private secured elevators and foyers, and walls of floor-to-ceiling glass framing the bay. Interiors by Florentine designer Michele Bönan pair tropical nonchalance with European craftsmanship — bespoke Molteni&C kitchens, Calacatta and Carrara marble baths over Italian travertine — while the architecture's soft curves follow the coastal silhouette. Residents draw on full Four Seasons service and a deep amenity package: a serviced pool deck and bar lounge, an intimate library, a spa with a Roman-thermae-inspired Caesar Experience moving through five contrast-therapy rooms, an indoor pool, Finnish saunas, and a fitness center and yoga studio, with a signature restaurant offering in-residence dining and a private landscaped setting by Raymond Jungles among the neighborhood's banyan canopy.
Architect: Revuelta Architecture International, Michele Bönan, Raymond Jungles

StatusHistory.note: CMC Group and Fort Partners closed a $323.8M construction loan from Bank OZK
Neighborhood backfill: Coconut Grove (per project name + official site)

Title: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Palm Beach Gardens
Description: A 106-residence Ritz-Carlton–branded waterfront community on the last 14 contiguous acres of Intracoastal frontage in the Palm Beach Gardens Marina District, with a 95-foot infinity pool, a 29-slip private marina, and 20,000+ square feet of resort amenities.
DescriptionLong: Set on the last 14 contiguous acres of Intracoastal waterfront in the Palm Beach Gardens Marina District, The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Palm Beach Gardens brings branded, service-driven living to one of South Florida's fastest-rising waterfront markets. Three buildings hold 106 residences wrapped by more than 20,000 square feet of resort amenities — a 95-foot infinity pool over the Intracoastal with twelve private cabanas and food, beverage, and towel service, a waterfront pavilion with outdoor grilling, pickleball courts, a dog park, and waterfront walking paths. Above the marina, an upper amenity terrace adds green space nearly the size of two football fields, a putting green, and sweeping water views, with select residences offering direct access and private terraces topping 1,800 square feet. Indoors, residents draw on the Lighthouse Lounge, a grand lobby with 24-hour front desk, a waterfront fitness center, and a private clubhouse with bar and dining, alongside 24/7 concierge and Ritz-Carlton anticipatory service and boating from a private 29-slip marina. The large-format Estate Collection residences span more than 3,800 square feet in three- to five-bedroom flow-through layouts with dens, oversized chef's kitchens with Wolf and Sub-Zero appliances, spa-style primary suites, walk-in pantries, and generous terraces for indoor-outdoor living along the water.
Architect: Spina O'Rourke + Partners, Susurrus, Decorators Unlimited

StatusHistory.note: Summer 2026 opening per Florida YIMBY (date confirm)
The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Palm Beach Gardens topped off in January 2025.

Title: 346 Madison Avenue
Description: A 46-story, all-electric office tower rising a block from Grand Central and across from One Vanderbilt, with column-free floors, terrace and loggia levels, and a two-floor amenity collection including a 215-seat auditorium, a Daniel Boulud épicerie, and an indoor padel court.
DescriptionLong: A new 46-story, all-electric office tower planned for one of the best development sites in midtown Manhattan — a block from Grand Central Terminal and directly across from One Vanderbilt, on the site of the former Brooks Brothers flagship. The roughly 850,000-square-foot tower trades a center core for a side core, opening up column-free floorplates with ceilings from 15 to 22 feet, nine floors with private terraces, and nine more wrapped in loggias and oversized glass, targeting the highest tiers of sustainability and WELL certification. A two-floor amenity collection includes a 215-seat auditorium, a tenant lounge anchored by an épicerie from Michelin-starred chef Daniel Boulud, and a landscaped terrace over Madison Avenue, alongside a luxury wellness center with a tenant-only fitness club, spa-quality locker rooms, a regulation-size indoor padel court billed as the only one inside an office building, and a world-class restaurant planned at the ground floor.

Title: Nauka
Description: A private 920-acre community on Mexico's Pacific coast built around a Tom Fazio championship golf course with three oceanfront holes you can swim mid-round, anchored by a Ritz-Carlton Reserve and limited to just 395 residences.
DescriptionLong: A private, low-density community spanning 920 acres along the Riviera Nayarit coast, 45 minutes north of Puerto Vallarta, built around one of the most ambitious golf experiences in the Americas. At its heart is Nauka Golf Club, a Tom Fazio par-72, 7,600-yard championship course that threads through four distinct worlds — old-growth jungle, volcanic cliffs, a mangrove estuary, and more than four miles of Pacific coastline — with five sets of tees and a signature run of three consecutive oceanfront holes where players are invited to go barefoot and wade into the surf mid-round. On-course dining anchors the round at The Nest, a cliffside comfort station high above the Pacific, while a lighted nine-hole par-3 short course, The Booby Trap, is built for night play and social rounds. The wider community reaches far past golf: a beach club with a 197-foot infinity pool, a marina village and yacht club, a 15-acre jungle wellness sanctuary, ocean and racquet clubs, an organic farm driving the culinary program, and an adventure roster from surfing and spearfishing to zip lines and climbing. Just 395 residences — Beach Estates with direct Pacific access, elevated Cliff Estates, and four-bedroom Golf Villas along the 18th fairway — keep it intensely private, with a Ritz-Carlton Reserve anchoring the resort core.

Title: MIRAI Design District
Description: Kengo Kuma's first U.S. mixed-use development — a 'floating lantern' of 41,000 sf of boutique office and 16,000 sf of luxury retail and hospitality, breaking ground on NE 2nd Avenue in the Design District.
DescriptionLong: MIRAI Design District is the first mixed-use development in the United States from internationally acclaimed Japanese architect Kengo Kuma and Associates, rising at 4218 NE 2nd Avenue in Miami's Design District. The project introduces approximately 41,000 square feet of Class A boutique office space — positioned for family offices, small and mid-sized funds, and design-led businesses — alongside 16,000 square feet of luxury high-street retail and hospitality space, all conceived as a "floating lantern" with a transparent ground floor, layered façade screening system, lush central garden, and hospitality-driven public spaces. Landscape architecture is led by Island Planning Corporation under Nathan Browning, with celebrated Italian lighting firm Viabizzuno designing a dynamic façade lighting program. Developed by a consortium of Leviathan Development (led by founder Allison Greenfield), Lionheart Capital (led by founder and CEO Ophir Sternberg), Well Duo, and The Lane Organization, with WD Capital Group advising on the capitalization. Ground broke in June 2026 following the closing of an $85 million construction loan from Monroe Capital LLC; completion is anticipated in 2028.
Developer: Leviathan Development, Lionheart Capital, WellDuo, The Lane Organization

StatusHistory.note: 3-floor mixed-use (ground retail + 2 office floors), no residential; broke ground w/ $85M loan June 2026 per TRD
MIRAI secured an $85M construction loan from Monroe Capital and started construction on Kengo Kuma's first U.S. mixed-use project
Sant Ambroeus signed as a restaurant tenant at MIRAI in the Miami Design District
MIRAI secures $85M construction loan from Monroe Capital as Kengo Kuma's first U.S. project breaks ground
Located in Miami's Design District (per project name + official site)

Title: Flow at Aventura Corporate Center
Description: Two 32-story residential towers — one condo, one rental — under the Flow lifestyle brand, rising on a reimagined Biscayne Boulevard office campus with ground-floor retail, shared amenities, and a fleet of community cars and bikes.
DescriptionLong: A pair of 32-story towers, rising to 367 feet, planned on the 8.6-acre Aventura Corporate Center along Biscayne Boulevard, adding 720 residences to a site that keeps its three existing office buildings in place. One tower would hold condominiums and the other rentals, both carrying the Flow residential brand, connected by a five-story podium with four levels of parking and ground-floor retail and amenities. Homes would range from roughly 550 to 1,494 square feet across one- to four-bedroom layouts, with a mix of 298 one-bedrooms, 271 two-bedrooms, 105 three-bedrooms, and 46 four-bedrooms. The plan folds in 20 units of workforce "hero housing" for city employees, first responders, and teachers, a shared fleet of 25 community cars and 50 bikes for residents, and a commitment to bar short-term rentals — an office-park-to-residential conversion of the kind reshaping Miami-Dade.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Aventura (per project name)

Title: Pine Crest School West Palm Beach
Description: A planned third campus for Pine Crest School, the 1934-founded South Florida independent day school, bringing its pre-K–12 model of academics, STEM, arts, and athletics to a fast-growing West Palm Beach.
DescriptionLong: Pine Crest School, the independent day school founded in 1934 with established campuses in Fort Lauderdale and Boca Raton, plans to open its third campus in West Palm Beach. The school is known for a college-preparatory model that pairs a strong academic foundation with design-thinking and entrepreneurial programs, alongside STEM, fine arts, and athletics across pre-kindergarten through grade twelve. The new campus is intended to extend that experience to families drawn by the region's rapid growth, with enhanced spaces for research, technology, entrepreneurship, engineering, artificial intelligence, and experiential learning. Specific location, grade levels, and opening timeline are still being finalized.

Title: The Brook Tennis and Pickleball
DescriptionLong:
The Brook Tennis and Pickleball is a roughly 79,000-square-foot indoor-and-outdoor racquet sports facility opening in suburban Boynton Beach in summer 2026 at 8255 Boynton Beach Boulevard, Building A. The climate-controlled venue offers five indoor tennis courts and two outdoor tennis courts, plus 14 indoor pickleball courts and six outdoor pickleball courts — all indoor courts air-conditioned for year-round play in a region where heat and rain routinely limit outdoor scheduling. Amenities include professional-grade lighting, designated viewing areas, a full-service pro shop, locker rooms, and on-site food and beverage including a café and sports bar. The Brook is owned and operated by Glenbrook Racquet Sports, a Chicago-based organization with more than two decades in the business that runs one of the country's largest indoor tennis clubs and previously developed what it bills as the country's first purpose-built indoor pickleball facility. The Boynton Beach location is the company's first fully indoor tennis and pickleball venue in Florida. Founding memberships began selling in 2025, with the first 100 already sold out ahead of the planned summer 2026 opening.</description_long>
<parameter name="developer_names">["Glenbrook Racquet Sports"]
ConfigSummary: ~79,000 sq ft indoor/outdoor racquet club · 14 indoor + 6 outdoor pickleball courts · 5 indoor + 2 outdoor tennis courts · café + sports bar

Title: Atelier Residences Miami
Description: A 10-story, 148-unit boutique mixed-use condominium in The Roads designed for short-term rental ownership, anchored by the CAYA wellness members' club.
DescriptionLong: Atelier Residences Miami is a 10-story, 148-unit boutique mixed-use condominium rising on a 0.8-acre site at 1265 SW 22nd Street, in the leafy historic Roads neighborhood just west of Brickell. Designed by Gensler with E. Frances Architects and interiors by Pfeifer Haus, the building introduces a contemporary glass-and-textured-panel facade integrated into the residential scale of its surroundings, with floor-to-ceiling windows, terraces, and landscaped rooftop and terrace-level open spaces. The 148 turnkey, fully furnished residences range from studios to three-bedroom layouts spanning roughly 360 to 1,500 square feet, with smart-home integration, keyless entry, and integrated appliance packages — pricing starts in the mid-$400,000s. Atelier is purpose-built for the short-term rental investor, with a centralized management program returning 90 percent of average daily rate to owners and an HOA structure that bundles service, security, maintenance, and check-in. Roughly 15,000 square feet of amenities anchor the lifestyle program: rooftop pool and sun deck, fitness studio, lobby, on-site restaurant and breakfast venue, a ghost kitchen for in-residence dining, 24/7 concierge and valet — and CAYA (Come As You Are), a wellness members' club spanning health, wellness, nourishment, and social pillars with spa, IV therapy, hot-cold treatments, training programs, and curated programming. Developed by Miami-based BLDG Ventures, led by founder and CEO René Bello, on a site owned by investor Carlos Alaimo's Delia Hospitality. Sales and marketing led by DMT.
Architect: Gensler, E Frances Architects, Pfeifer Haus

Title: Aston Martin Residences Daytona Beach Shores
Description: An oceanfront Aston Martin–branded residential tower on the Atlantic coast, with 86 residences crowned by double-height penthouses, fine dining, an artisan bakery, and direct access to one of the world's most famous beaches.
DescriptionLong: An ultra-luxury oceanfront tower carrying the Aston Martin name, rising on the Atlantic coast just minutes from the Daytona International Speedway and overlooking the sweep of Daytona Beach Shores. The 18-floor, roughly 320,000-square-foot building holds 86 residences shaped by the British marque's design language — the highly crafted, beautifully proportioned forms and cutting-edge detailing drawn from its automotive models — and is topped by two penthouse floors with eight double-height residences. Every home draws on a world-class amenity package, while the mixed-use base brings fine dining and an artisan bakery, and the beachfront setting offers residents direct access to the sand. Two parking floors anchor the lower levels of the tower.

Title: 1 Iris Lane, The Residences at Belle Meade Village
Description: A pair of boutique residential buildings at the gateway to Belle Meade — 92 one- to four-bedroom condominiums averaging about 3,200 square feet, wrapped by a five-acre private park and 16,000 square feet of amenities.
DescriptionLong: The only for-sale residential offering within the 15.5-acre Belle Meade Village development, 1 Iris Lane comprises two boutique buildings holding 92 condominiums in one- to four-bedroom layouts averaging roughly 3,200 square feet, with marble finishes, terraces framing views from the retail village to Richland Creek, 24-hour concierge, and full lifestyle staff. Residents have access to 16,000 square feet of amenity space — a library, piano room, screening and listening rooms, a golf simulator, a Pilates studio, a private pool and spa, an on-site salon, wine, whiskey, and cigar rooms, and entertainment spaces from intimate lounges to private mahjong and poker rooms. The buildings are surrounded by the five-acre Treetops Park & Gardens, with landscaped paths connecting to the Town Square, the cosmopolitan retail village, and the restored Richland Creek greenway.
Architect: Hartshorne Plunkard Architecture, Hastings Architecture, Meyer Davis

StatusHistory.note: Two ~8-floor buildings × 46 units = 92 per Nashville Post (floors were 10)
Site blasting complete; vertical concrete construction underway on The Residences at Belle Meade Village
Vertical construction underway at the Belle Meade Village site after site prep and deep foundations

Title: Dolly Parton's SongTeller Hotel
Description: Dolly Parton's 12-story downtown Nashville hotel, with 245 music-themed rooms and suites, two live-music venues, a Listening Lobby, and a 20,000-square-foot museum of her life and career.
DescriptionLong: A 12-story downtown Nashville hotel just off Lower Broadway, conceived as Dolly Parton's love letter to Music City and marked by a guitar-shaped entryway. The 245 music-themed rooms and suites range from Signature Rooms to Acoustic Suites, the six-guest Six Sisters Suites honoring Dolly's sisters, and a two-story SongTeller Suite, with signature touches like in-room guitars and record players. A rainbow-hued Listening Lobby with gilded ceilings plays Dolly's songs every hour and hosts songwriting sessions, while two venues — Parton's Live, a performance space for songwriter sessions and rising-star showcases, and Jolene's, a piano lounge — bring live music under the same roof alongside craft cocktails and elevated fare. The hotel's third floor houses Dolly's Life of Many Colors Museum, a 20,000-plus-square-foot exhibit billed as the largest celebration of her life and career in the world, steps from the Ryman Auditorium, Bridgestone Arena, and the heart of Lower Broadway.

StatusHistory.note: Opening pulled to June 2026 per Islands (date confirm)

Title: Belle Meade Village
Description: A cosmopolitan shopping and residential village at the gateway to Belle Meade, with 40-plus storefronts, a members' club, a boutique hotel, dining, and wellness arranged around a courtyard anchored by an antique carousel.
DescriptionLong: A mixed-use shopping and residential village transforming a 15.5-acre site at the gateway to Belle Meade, at the entrance to one of Nashville's most established residential corridors. Comprised of seven two- and three-story buildings arranged around a central courtyard anchored by an antique carousel, the village brings together more than 40 storefronts, a members' club, a boutique hotel, health and wellness, cafés, and world-class restaurants. Green space, public walkways, and parks make up roughly 60% of the site, including the activation of Richland Creek and a connection to its namesake greenway that links to the pedestrian-friendly Sylvan Park neighborhood and McCabe Park.

StatusHistory.note: Residential buildings stand ~8 floors per Nashville Post; vertical construction underway
Excavation blasting wrapped (50 ft below grade for a 900-space garage); vertical concrete construction now underway
AJ Capital Partners secured a $475M construction loan for the mixed-use project
Site blasting complete; excavation, deep foundations and vertical construction now underway. Includes 78 hotel rooms
AJ Capital Partners closed $475M construction loan for the mixed-use project

Title: NoBe PARC Miami Beach
Description: A 15-story North Beach tower of 232 fully furnished, short-term-rental-friendly residences a short walk from the sand, with resort-style amenities and turnkey homes ready to live in or lease from day one.
DescriptionLong: A 15-story residential tower bringing 232 fully furnished residences to Miami Beach's North Beach neighborhood, on a 1.24-acre site steps from the shoreline. Floor plans range from studios and junior one-bedrooms to three-bedroom-plus-den layouts spanning roughly 375 to 849 square feet, each delivered move-in ready with private terraces, custom Italian-inspired cabinetry, quartz countertops, full appliance packages, glass-enclosed showers, built-out closets, and in-unit laundry. Short-term-rental-friendly zoning lets owners live in, lease, or seasonally use their homes from day one. A fifth-floor amenity deck centers on a resort-style pool with integrated spa and sun shelves, plus a wellness suite of sauna, steam room, treatment room, and outdoor cold plunge, alongside co-working spaces, social lounges, summer kitchens, gaming lawns, and a children's playroom. The rooftop deck adds sunrise yoga space, cabanas, and lounges overlooking the water.

StatusHistory.note: Completion now end of 2028 (was 2029-03) per Florida YIMBY/Hoodline; 232 units, 15 floors confirmed.
Neighborhood backfill: North Beach (NoBe), Miami Beach (per project name + official site)

Title: Coleman Park
Description: A mixed-income community hub rising along the Tamarind corridor with townhomes, rental apartments, commercial space, a fresh-market grocery, and a cultural gathering yard anchored by a library.
DescriptionLong: A neighborhood-scale revitalization of the historic Coleman Park area, reactivating the Tamarind Avenue corridor with mixed-income housing and a community common known as the Culture Yard. The plan calls for townhomes alongside a mixed-use building combining rental apartments with ground-floor commercial space dedicated to community-serving uses like childcare and healthcare. A fresh-market grocery and a cultural gathering space anchored by a library complete the vision, with early upgrades including a colorful library structure and new bus shelters already taking shape on site.
Developer: Palm Beach Venture Philanthropy, Quantum Foundation, Neighborhood Renaissance

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Coleman Park (per project name + our WPB coverage)

Title: The Breakers Family Entertainment Center
Description: A 46,800-square-foot family entertainment center rising at The Breakers Palm Beach, nearly quadrupling the resort's current facility with indoor and outdoor attractions, casual dining, and immersive programming for guests and club members.
DescriptionLong: The Breakers Palm Beach is replacing its 12,100-square-foot family entertainment center with a 46,800-square-foot destination — a nearly fourfold expansion of one of the resort's most beloved spaces. The new center weaves together indoor and outdoor attractions, casual dining, and immersive programming designed for families and members alike, building on a beloved lineup that has long included an arcade, a nine-hole mini golf course, a basketball court, laser tag, and a playground. Set within the historic 140-acre oceanfront estate, the center anchors a wider reinvention of the property and is targeted to open in spring 2028.

StatusHistory.note: The Breakers began demolition to replace its 12,100 sf family facility with a 46,800 sf entertainment center; opening targeted spring 2028

Title: Meliá Residences Miami
Description: Meliá Hotels & Resorts' first hotel-branded residential concept in North America, a fully furnished, turnkey condominium just west of Brickell that topped off in May 2026.
DescriptionLong: Meliá Residences Miami marks Meliá Hotels & Resorts' return to Miami and its first hotel-branded residential concept in North America, rising at 1120 SW 3rd Avenue on the western edge of Brickell. Each residence is conceived as a turnkey home — delivered fully furnished and move-in ready — with rooms designed by Concepto Taller de Arquitectura and residential interiors and amenity spaces by Adriana Hoyos Design Studio, working in a Mediterranean-inspired palette of natural materials and organic textures that nods to the brand's heritage. The amenity program leans into Meliá's global lifestyle platform: wellness and relaxation spaces, Mediterranean-inspired fitness facilities, pools and outdoor terraces, social lounges, coworking areas, and hospitality-driven resident and guest services. Developed by Urban Network Capital Group (UNCG) in partnership with Meliá Hotels & Resorts and Concepto Capital Group, with a $30 million construction loan arranged by Concord Summit Capital.
Architect: Adriana Hoyos Design Studio, Concepto Taller De Arquitectura, DTI Architects
Developer: Urban Network Capital Group, Melia Hotels Resorts, Concepto Capital Group

StatusHistory.note: Meliá Residences Miami topped off in Brickell, with the final beam placed May 21, 2026.

Title: 120 S. Dixie Highway
DescriptionLong:
Billionaire developer Jeff Greene has proposed a 25-story Live Local Act apartment tower at 120 S. Dixie Highway in downtown West Palm Beach — set to become the tallest mass timber building in Florida. Designed by Carlo Ratti Associati (the Turin- and New York-based practice whose founder curated the 2025 Venice Architecture Biennale) and engineered with a pre-fabricated mass timber system by Nexus Systems, the 0.87-acre project assembles 120 S. Dixie Highway with 321, 324, 325, and 333 Datura Street. The development delivers up to 399 apartments (studios to three-bedrooms, ranging 505 to 1,320 sq ft), 7,557 sq ft of retail, and 236 parking spaces. Filed under Florida's Live Local Act, approximately 160 units (40%) are reserved as workforce housing for residents earning up to 120% of area median income (set at $104,000 for Palm Beach County), with workforce units on floors 2–4 and 6–11 and market-rate units on floors 12 and up. The historic 1915 fire station on site will be incorporated into the first floor of the tower. A fifth-floor amenity deck features a pool, fitness room, lounge, yoga room, private dining area, and grilling stations. Unlike many Live Local Act projects, the building runs a single shared lobby and shared amenity deck across all income tiers. The project went before the city's Plans & Plats Review Committee on May 14, 2026 and was returned for changes; Live Local Act approval routes through city administration rather than board vote.</description_long>
<parameter name="developer_names">["Jeff Greene"]

StatusHistory.note: Latest plan: 25-story mass-timber tower with 366 apartments (148 workforce units, 40%); in pre-filing
Jeff Greene's 25-story mass-timber tower now planned for 399 apartments, 40% workforce housing

Title: 400 Lake Shore Drive
Description: A two-tower luxury residential development rising on the former Chicago Spire site where the Chicago River meets Lake Michigan, led by an 858-foot, 72-story north tower that just topped out.
DescriptionLong: 400 Lake Shore Drive is a transformative two-tower residential development rising on the long-vacant former Chicago Spire site, at the confluence of the Chicago River and Lake Michigan in Streeterville. The first-phase north tower topped out in April 2026 at 858 feet and 72 stories, delivering 635 rental residences — 127 of them (20%) designated affordable — with completion and move-ins targeted for early 2027. Designed by the Chicago office of Skidmore, Owings & Merrill with David Childs and Scott Duncan, the towers feature cascading terraces down their eastern facades and glass-and-metal curtain walls inspired by the classic "Chicago window," angled to frame views of Lake Michigan. Interiors are by MAWD (March and White Design), whose Related portfolio includes Row Fulton Market. Beyond housing, the development adds roughly 4.5 acres of public open space, delivers the long-awaited 3.3-acre DuSable Park (backed by a $10 million pledge), and completes the north end of Chicago's Riverwalk via Founder's Way. A planned second phase adds a south tower of approximately 765 feet and 64 stories with around 465 residences, still in the proposed stage with timing to be announced; the two towers share roughly 300 underground parking spaces. Developed by Related Midwest with general contractors LR Contracting Company and BOWA Construction.

StatusHistory.note: North Tower completion early 2027 per Chicago YIMBY (date confirm)
400 Lake Shore held its official groundbreaking ceremony on June 17, 2024.

Title: Nude Miami
DescriptionLong: Miami's first boutique organic grocer and café, open at 1100 Brickell Bay Drive on the ground floor of Panorama Tower in Brickell. Founded by three Miami locals — Charles Amine, Harry Miller, and Sebastian Lezcano — the 4,720-square-foot space is built around a strict ingredient-first standard: no GMOs, no seed oils, and no artificial colors, flavors, or preservatives. The flagship features Miami's first organic, seed-oil-free hot bar with grass-fed steak, wild-caught salmon, and pasture-raised chicken, alongside a full café serving smoothies, matcha, cold-pressed juices, and pastries from local bakeries. Operations run on the Vori grocery-management platform, built to support a multi-location rollout, with additional South Florida locations already in development.

Title: The Carnelian
Description: A five-star boutique hotel rising near Waterside Shops, anchored by Sterling's Club — a 70,000-sf private members' club opening across three floors in late 2027.
DescriptionLong: The Carnelian is a five-star boutique hotel now under construction in the heart of Naples, positioned steps from Waterside Shops and minutes from Seagate Beach. The building is anchored by Sterling's Club, the city's first hospitality-led private members' club, occupying the second, sixth, and rooftop floors with more than 70,000 square feet of member-only experiences opening in late 2027. Sterling's brings a fully realized lifestyle ecosystem under one roof: a 23,000-square-foot Wellness Club with rooftop lap pool, hydrotherapy zone, infrared sauna, and hammam steam room; a collection of dining venues including Riva (rooftop pool restaurant), The Dining Room, The Champagne Bar, The Club Room, The Drawing Room library retreat, Jetlag speakeasy, and Boulevardier cigar lounge; plus nightly entertainment programming developed in partnership with Artis–Naples and Gulfshore Playhouse. Pre-construction memberships are active, with founding members already shaping the club's culture ahead of opening.

StatusHistory.note: 70-suite boutique hotel (keys), 6 stories + rooftop level; Sterling's Club/Carnelian debut late 2026 (was 2027-12)
70-suite boutique hotel (keys), 6 stories + rooftop level; Sterling's Club/Carnelian debut late 2026 (was 2027-12)
Spec fill/correct: six-story hotel with 70 suites (keys=70, floors 7→6); delivery left as-is (sources conflict late-2026 vs late-2027)

Title: The Galleria at Fort Lauderdale
Description: A phased redevelopment of the 31.5-acre Galleria mall near East Sunrise Boulevard into a mixed-use district of up to eight 342-foot towers with 3,144 apartments, a 170-room hotel, and 1M+ square feet of commercial space.
DescriptionLong: The Galleria at Fort Lauderdale is set for a sweeping phased redevelopment that would transform the roughly 31.5-acre regional mall property near East Sunrise Boulevard and the Intracoastal Waterway into a high-density mixed-use district. In May 2026, the FAA issued "Determination of No Hazard to Air Navigation" status for eight proposed towers labeled B1 through B8, each rising 342 feet above ground level — approximately 30 stories — though various reports indicate the plan could include up to nine towers (two of which are attached and sometimes shown separately). Plans submitted to the City of Fort Lauderdale in August 2025 call for 3,144 rental apartments, including 1,273 workforce housing units proposed under Florida's Live Local Act, a 170-room hotel, office and retail space, restaurants, more than 4,700 parking spaces, and over 1 million square feet of retained and new commercial, retail, restaurant, wellness, and office space. An early retail anchor is confirmed: Wayfair will open its first large-format Florida store here in late 2027, spanning roughly 94,000 square feet across two levels.
ProjectType: Mixed-Use, Residences, Hotel, Retail, Office, Eateries
Developer: GFO Investments, Insite Group, Atlas Hill Real Estate, Prime Finance

StatusHistory.note: Redevelopment includes a 170-room hotel alongside 3,144 units in eight 342-ft towers; FAA determinations issued May 2026 per Florida YIMBY.

Title: H.O.R.I.Z.O.N.
DescriptionLong: H.O.R.I.Z.O.N. is a proposed public-private redevelopment of Lake Worth Beach's city-owned beachfront and municipal golf course, submitted by an investment group led by Copperline Partners and projected to generate $150M+ a year for the local economy and roughly 1,000 permanent jobs. The plan keeps land ownership with the city under long-term leases and centers on two Hyatt-branded hotels: a $105M, 250-room Grand Hyatt on the beachside parking lots and a $95M, 150-room Hyatt conference hotel at the golf course. The municipal course would get a $22M redesign by Jack Nicklaus' new firm, 1JN, alongside a 5-acre oceanfront park, a $50M parking garage, and new beachside buildings with restaurants, shops, and a marine-themed attraction. The Intracoastal side adds a kayak and paddleboard launch, with a new city recreation center and pool built inland at Northwest Park.
ProjectType: Hotel, Golf, Park, Eateries, Retail, Entertainment, Marina
ConfigSummary: Two Hyatt hotels (250-key Grand Hyatt beachside + 150-key conference hotel at golf course), Nicklaus/1JN golf redesign, 5-acre oceanfront park, parking garage, beachside retail/dining

Title: Marina Village
Description: A proposed $556 million phased waterfront development at Riviera Beach's Marina Village, anchored by a 20-story Marriott Autograph Collection hotel, a 20-story condo with boat storage, and a possible convention center.
DescriptionLong: Forest Development, led by Peter Baytarian, won a Riviera Beach city council vote in May 2026 for the right to build a proposed $556.1 million phased development at Marina Village, part of an 80-acre city-owned waterfront assemblage that officials have sought to redevelop for over a decade. The North Palm Beach–based firm beat out The Related Group and Sonnenblick Development for roughly 12 acres of the site, though it must still finalize an agreement with the city before moving forward. The proposal includes a 20-story Marriott Autograph Collection–branded hotel, a possible 10-story convention center (with London-based Informa, operator of the Fort Lauderdale International Boat Show, expressing interest in running it), a 20-story condominium with boat storage, three three-story restaurant and office buildings, a revamped Bicentennial Park, roughly 2,300 parking spaces, and a culinary school run by celebrity chef Richard Burke offering scholarships to Riviera Beach residents. West Palm Beach–based Okeechobee Steakhouse is slated to occupy at least one restaurant building. The development team includes architecture firms Adache and Dwell, planning firms Insite Studio and WGI, and general contractors Moss and Gibane. Funding is tied to Forest Development's $300 million in active loans. Note: this is a winning bid at the pre-agreement stage, not an entitled or approved project; a related FBI investigation involving subpoenaed Marina Village documents adds uncertainty to the timeline.
ProjectType: Mixed-Use, Hotel, Residences, Eateries, Education, Park

StatusHistory.note: Forest Development selected for the $556M Marina Village redevelopment; plan adds a 270-key Marriott Autograph hotel and 164 condos
Forest Development selected for the $556M Marina Village redevelopment; plan adds a 270-key Marriott Autograph hotel and 164 condos

Title: YMCA of the Palm Beaches
Description: A $75 million, 55,000-square-foot flagship YMCA community campus rising at Lake Lytal Park, anchoring an Olympic-style skate park and a new Palm Beach County aquatic center along Southern Boulevard.
DescriptionLong: The new YMCA of the Palm Beaches flagship is a 55,000-square-foot, two-story community center under construction at Lake Lytal Park, along the highly visible Southern Boulevard corridor in central Palm Beach County near Palm Beach International Airport. Delivered through a public-private partnership between the YMCA of the Palm Beaches and Palm Beach County Parks & Recreation — enabled by a 50-year county ground lease — the facility triples the size of the organization's current branch and is projected to serve an estimated 18,000 residents annually. The community center is designed to function well beyond a traditional gym, with advanced wellness facilities, a teen Youth Engagement and Resource Center, STEM and robotics labs, a music/recording studio, art studio, teaching kitchen, Y PreSchool Academy, and flexible childcare and after-school spaces. It anchors a broader $75 million, roughly 13-acre campus that also includes an Olympic-style skate park designed by Team Pain and Palm Beach County's new aquatic center — featuring a 70-meter competition pool with a 13-foot dive well and a 6,975-square-foot aquatic play zone with three slides. A Phase II expansion will add a basketball gym, indoor track, and additional youth-focused amenities. Designed by PBK Architects with Hedrick Brothers Construction as general contractor; completion targeted for late 2026 into 2027.

StatusHistory.note: Construction tracking to finish by end of 2026 with grand opening expected January 2027

Title: Inscription
Description: A new boutique-style luxury rental community on Presidential Way overlooking the 10th tee of a Jack Nicklaus–designed championship golf course, now leasing.
DescriptionLong: A rental community set between I-95 and Lake Mangonia, 1.5 miles from downtown West Palm Beach. Apartments span one-, two-, and three-bedroom layouts from approximately 760 to 1,485 square feet, finished with quartz countertops, stainless steel appliances, and chef-inspired kitchens. The boutique-style community overlooks the 10th tee of a renowned Jack Nicklaus–designed championship golf course, Dutchman's Pipe. Amenities include a resort-style pool, a well-equipped fitness center, and a rooftop terrace with a pizza oven, within a gated, pet-friendly setting. Now open and actively leasing.

Title: The Belgrove Resort & Spa
Description: A British Caribbean–inspired Marriott Autograph Collection resort on a former mango grove along Lake Mangonia, anchored by a spa, three pools, and a Jack Nicklaus Signature golf experience.
DescriptionLong: The Belgrove Resort & Spa, West Palm Beach, Autograph Collection is a luxury resort blending British Caribbean charm and West Indies–inspired architecture with modern sophistication minutes from downtown West Palm Beach. Guest rooms and suites feature private balconies, bespoke decor, and pillow-top bedding, while the resort's culinary program spans Society 48 (an American brasserie), a poolside retreat, a grab-and-go café, and in-room dining. Leisure amenities include three outdoor pools with striped cabanas, a full-service spa drawing on Caribbean healing traditions and the property's mango heritage, a 24-hour fitness center, a free beach shuttle, and 21,000 square feet of indoor-outdoor event space. The resort connects to a broader lifestyle offering: a new private members' club, Dutchman's Pipe, featuring a 7,300-yard Jack Nicklaus Signature golf course, PGA-level instruction academy, and a racquet center with tennis, pickleball, and padel.
ProjectType: Resort, Hotel, Spa, Golf, Eateries, Residences

Title: Amanvari
DescriptionLong:
Amanvari is Aman's first resort in Mexico, opening August 1, 2026 on the East Cape of Baja California within the 1,500-acre Costa Palmas estate near Los Cabos. The intimate 18-key resort — comprising standalone casitas, some with cantilevered private pools — sits at the meeting point of three landscapes: beachfront, desert, and estuary, with views of the Sea of Cortés and the Sierra de la Laguna mountains. Designed by Heah & Co. with Elastic Architects, the resort is built from white concrete, natural stone, and tropical hardwoods in a sand-hued palette. A beachfront main pavilion houses three restaurants — including Aman's Italian concept Arva and Japanese concept Sesui/Nama — plus a library, lounge, and infinity pool encircled by four jacuzzis. The Aman Spa features a modern temazcal, signature Spa Houses, and an open-air yoga pavilion. A collection of 24 Aman Residences is offered for sale, some overlooking the Robert Trent Jones II-designed golf course. Costa Palmas amenities include three miles of swimmable beach, a yacht club, and a deep-water marina. Amanvari becomes Aman's sixth property in the Americas and Caribbean.</description_long>
<parameter name="keys">18
ProjectType: Resort, Hotel, Residences, Eateries, Spa, Golf, Marina
ConfigSummary: 18-key Aman resort + 24 branded residences · Costa Palmas estate · Robert Trent Jones II golf course · opening August 1, 2026

Title: Southbank Residences
DescriptionLong:
Southbank Residences is a $200 million, landmark mixed-use development that broke ground on May 20, 2026 along Jacksonville's St. Johns River — the city's first new luxury high-rise on the downtown riverfront in more than a decade. Developed by Related Group and designed by internationally acclaimed architect Carlos Ott (Paris's Opéra Bastille), with MSA Architects as record architect and interiors by ID & Design International, the project delivers 395 rental residences across two connected towers: the 25-story Icon Southbank and the eight-story Manor Southbank. The development is anchored by a 4,500-square-foot waterfront restaurant activating the Riverwalk and Friendship Park, a 29-slip marina, a public boat ramp, and 601 structured parking spaces. Resort-style amenities include a spa and wellness center with cold plunge and saunas, a waterfront pool, speakeasy, library, golf simulator, private theater, fitness center, sky lounge, and a penthouse-reserved rooftop clubroom and terrace. Residents have direct access to the 1.25-mile Southbank Riverwalk. Rents start at $2,000/month for a studio to over $7,000 for a three-bedroom. Completion is expected in 2028–29.</description_long>
<parameter name="floors">25
Architect: Carlos Ott, MSA Architects, ID & Design International
ConfigSummary: $200M riverfront development · 395 residences across two towers · 4,500 sq ft waterfront restaurant · 29-slip marina

Title: Water Street Tampa Entertainment District
DescriptionLong:
Strategic Property Partners (SPP) has revealed a new entertainment district at Water Street Tampa, formally entering the City of Tampa's development review process. Anchored by a 3,500-capacity live music and entertainment venue operated in partnership with Vinik Sports Group and a 250-key hotel, the district extends the Water Street Tampa model into entertainment-focused placemaking. The venue features a 2,800-person general admission floor, a 700-person mezzanine, 8,000+ sq ft of exterior terrace overlooking Channelside Drive, and a 6,000 sq ft VIP lounge, operating on an independent "open room" booking model. The integrated hotel adds a 12,000 sq ft amenity and pool deck with direct VIP connectivity to the venue. The district also includes roughly 80,000 sq ft of entertainment-driven retail and dining across two levels, more than 25,000 sq ft of activated public realm, a central plaza facing Benchmark International Arena, and approximately 1,000 parking spaces. Designed by Gensler, with Moss Construction and Barton Malow as joint venture general contractors. Groundbreaking is anticipated in early 2027.</description_long>
<parameter name="architect_names">["Gensler"]
Developer: Strategic Property Partners, Vinik Sports Group
ConfigSummary: 3,500-capacity live music venue · 250-key hotel · 80,000 sq ft retail/dining · ~1,000 parking spaces

Title: The Society of the Four Arts
Description: A $250 million, 18-month renovation and expansion of two historic Mizner-era buildings on the Society of the Four Arts' 10-acre cultural campus along Royal Palm Way.
DescriptionLong: The Society of the Four Arts has begun a $250 million renovation and expansion of its 10-acre cultural campus in Midtown Palm Beach, focused on two historic buildings along Royal Palm Way just east of the Royal Park Bridge. The 18-month project overhauls the Esther B. O'Keeffe Gallery Building — originally Addison Mizner's Embassy Club and later reworked by John Volk — expanding its footprint from roughly 20,000 to nearly 31,000 square feet, enlarging and modernizing the galleries, and uncovering and restoring the original pecky cypress ceiling from Mizner's design. The adjacent Gubelmann Auditorium will gain improved acoustics, a larger stage, expanded backstage and dressing rooms, a catering area, and a loading dock, trading a modest seat reduction (700 to 650) for performance capability. The Rovensky Administrative Building — another Mizner design, built as the Embassy Apartment Building — receives a 10,356-square-foot, three-story addition, a relocated and expanded Children's Library with its own first-floor entrance and dedicated exhibition space (including works donated by Steve Wynn), new offices, and a 1,400-square-foot boardroom opening onto a 1,629-square-foot terrace. More than half of each building will be demolished while preserving their landmark facades. West Palm Beach–based Spina O'Rourke + Partners is the local architect of record, with Conkling & Lewis Construction handling the build. The King Library, Dixon Education Building, and Philip Hulitar Sculpture Garden remain open throughout. Funded entirely through trustee and member donations; completion targeted for late fall 2027, ahead of the 2027–28 season.

Title: Phoenix Arts & Innovation District
DescriptionLong: The Phoenix Arts & Innovation District (PHXJAX) is an 8.5-acre arts, innovation, and mixed-use campus taking shape in Jacksonville's historic Springfield neighborhood, just north of downtown along the 30-mile Emerald Trail. Developed by Future of Cities, founded by Tony Cho, in partnership with Live Oak Contracting, the project repurposes 120,000 square feet of historic industrial buildings into modern creative workspace while advancing a broader district with entitlements for up to 830 multifamily units — including affordable, workforce, and market-rate housing. Naked Kitchen opens May 20 as the first food and beverage tenant inside the district, with future phases adding residential and a grocer.
Developer: Future of Cities, Live Oak Estates Group

StatusHistory.note: Future of Cities and Blue Sky Communities land 9% LIHTC funding for $33M, 80-unit affordable housing building

Title: One West Twelve Residences
Description: A 23-story, 372-unit residential tower in Historic Overtown anchored by Vibe BBQ — a new restaurant and lounge concept from James Beard Award–winning chef Marcus Samuelsson.
DescriptionLong: One West Twelve Residences (ONE W12) is a 23-story, 245-foot residential tower delivering 372 fully furnished and finished residences in the heart of Miami's Historic Overtown — at the crossroads of Park West and the Arts & Entertainment District. The collection spans studios, one-bedrooms, and a limited group of one-, two-, and three-bedroom townhomes, all approved for short-term rental flexibility. Architecture by CUBE 3 with design by Cotofana Designs draws on the area's cultural and architectural heritage while introducing a modernist, storied glamour to the streetscape. Residences feature integrated kitchens with Miele appliances and Italkraft cabinetry, custom countertops and backsplashes, built-out closets, custom shades, modern bathroom fixtures, and integrated washer/dryer systems. Amenities include a rooftop garden and pool with lounge areas, a state-of-the-art fitness center, curated art installations, smart-building technology, an elegant lobby, and house cruiser bikes with storage. The ground floor will be anchored by Vibe BBQ — a quick-serve restaurant and lounge concept from eight-time James Beard Award winner Marcus Samuelsson, building on the success of his Red Rooster Overtown. Steps from MiamiCentral Station for Brightline and regional transit. Developed by Property Markets Group (PMG) in partnership with Lion Development Group and LNDMRK Development. General contractor John Moriarty & Associates broke ground October 1, 2025; completion anticipated 2028. The project was 97 percent sold at groundbreaking.
Developer: Property Markets Group, Lion Development Group, LNDMRK Development

Title: Palm Tree Residences Miami
DescriptionLong: The first-ever branded residence from Palm Tree Crew, the global entertainment and hospitality company co-founded by world-renowned music producer Kygo and Myles Shear. Developed by PMG, Lion Development Group, Eden Residential, and Sterling Equities in Miami's entertainment district, the project will deliver 483 fully furnished and finished residences — from junior suites to three-bedroom floorplans — designed by Kobi Karp Architecture & Interior Design with custom Italian Italkraft cabinetry and a fully integrated Miele appliance package. The three-level amenity collection features a ground-floor Palm Tree Crew dining and nightlife venue, a rooftop infinity-edge pool overlooking Downtown Miami, an outdoor padel court, fitness center, climbing wall, cold plunges, saunas, and a yoga studio. Ownership unlocks priority access to Palm Tree Festivals worldwide, weekday dining privileges at Palm Tree Club Miami, and members-only access across the Palm Tree Crew hospitality portfolio. Sales launch the first week of June ahead of the FIFA World Cup in Miami.
Developer: Property Markets Group, Lion Development Group, Eden Residential, Sterling Equities

StatusHistory.note: Tower spans 37 stories (483 units) per The Real Deal/Florida YIMBY — filling missing floors.

Title: Jean-Georges Miami Tropic Residences
Description: A 49-story, 338-unit luxury tower at the edge of the Miami Design District with culinary and lifestyle programming by two-Michelin-starred chef Jean-Georges Vongerichten.
DescriptionLong: Jean-Georges Miami Tropic Residences is a 49-story luxury condominium tower bringing 338 residences to the edge of Miami's Design District, within walkable reach of Wynwood, Midtown, and the neighborhood's anchor cultural institutions. Architecture by Arquitectonica with interiors by Yabu Pushelberg and landscape by CLAD shape a building immersed in tropical foliage, anchored by a private second-level porte-cochère and a ground floor housing Jean-Georges' inaugural abc kitchens in Miami. Two Michelin-starred chef Jean-Georges Vongerichten extends his culinary mastery across the building: residence kitchens are designed in his image, a residents-only indoor/outdoor restaurant anchors the pool deck on Level 8, and curated in-residence dining, catering menus, and pantry stocking carry the programming into private homes. Residences span Levels 9–47 with 10- to 11-foot floor-to-ceiling glass, private and semi-private elevator vestibules, and spacious balconies framing 180-degree views of Biscayne Bay, the Design District, and Downtown. The Penthouse Collection occupies Level 48, followed by a Rooftop Skydeck on Level 49 with pool, sky bar, archive library, and private dining room. Amenities also include a coworking floor with podcast studio and art gallery (Level 5), an indoor/outdoor fitness and spa floor with squash court and healing garden (Level 7), and resort-style pool deck with outdoor theater, golf simulator, and kids' programming (Level 8). Florida Green Building Coalition–aligned design. Developed by Terra and Lion, with exclusive sales by Douglas Elliman Development Marketing.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Design District (per coverage + official site)

Title: 1250 West Avenue
Description: A 330-foot waterfront condominium tower on West Avenue in South Beach, replacing a 1964 building with 106 boutique-scale residences averaging nearly 3,500 square feet.
DescriptionLong: A 330-foot waterfront condominium tower planned for South Beach's West Avenue corridor was unanimously approved by the Miami Beach Design Review Board in May 2026. The development replaces an existing 15-story, 238-unit condominium completed in 1964 with a boutique 106-unit tower designed by Hollywood-based ODP Architecture and Design. Residences average nearly 3,500 square feet, with two penthouses crowned by private rooftop pools and amenity decks. The ground floor adds 2,685 square feet of commercial space, underground parking, and tropical gardens maintained by pollutant-filtered bioswales. As part of the previously approved overlay zoning agreement, the development team will complete its segment of the Baywalk and redevelop the adjacent Bikini Hostel site at 1247 West Avenue into a city park.
Architect: Odp Architecture and Design, ODP Architects
Developer: Terra, RG Development Group, GV Development

StatusHistory.note: 28-story tower (330 ft), DRB approved May 2026; 106 units confirmed — filling missing floors per Florida YIMBY/The Real Deal.

Title: Fulton Bond
Description: A full-block luxury condominium development in Fulton Market, designed by KPF with ParkFowler Plus and interiors by MAWD, planned across two phased towers rising to 33 stories.
DescriptionLong: Fulton Bond is a full-block condominium development at 1325 West Fulton Street in Chicago's Fulton Market District, bounded by North Ada Street to the west and North Elizabeth Street to the east. Designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox (KPF) in collaboration with ParkFowler Plus, the architecture interprets Fulton Market's industrial heritage through hand-set and precast brick arches, articulated podiums, and inverted bay windows expressed on the exterior as masonry arches — a contextual response rooted in the neighborhood's warehouse character. Interiors by MAWD / March and White Design layer warmth, curvature, and tonal richness across residences and shared spaces conceived to evoke a private home rather than a conventional condominium. Latest revisions (February 2026) reshape the project into two phased towers above a shared podium: a first-phase eastern tower of approximately 25 stories with a corrugated crown, followed by a 33-story western tower of approximately 500 feet clad in dark gray brick — together delivering roughly 149 to 240 residences across two- to four-bedroom layouts and penthouses, ranging from about 2,400 to over 4,500 square feet. Pricing guidance starts at $1.5M and runs into the mid-$7M range for penthouses. Shared amenities include a residents lounge with bar, fitness and wellness center, interactive golf simulator, outdoor pool with landscaped terrace, dog run, and 24-hour concierge. Developed by Sulo Development, which received City Council approval in early 2025 and completed an on-site sales center in 2025; presales are underway with delivery anticipated 2027–2029.
Architect: Kohn Pedersen Fox, Parkfowler Plus, Mawd

StatusHistory.note: Two-tower project (23 + 33 stories), ~149 condos, targeting 2027 completion per Chicago YIMBY/The Real Deal — filling missing delivery.
Sulo Development secured a $102M construction loan to start the project, with Clark Construction as GC
Fulton Bond's first tower targets an August 2026 groundbreaking and 2028 delivery

Title: DUS Padel
DescriptionLong:
A new 26,000-square-foot indoor padel club coming to 2031 Okeechobee Blvd in West Palm Beach, founded by Palm Beach County locals and designed as a hospitality-driven destination at the intersection of sport, wellness, and community. The club will feature five weather-protected indoor courts open to members and non-members, an organic café, men's and women's locker rooms with saunas and cold plunges, a spa-inspired recovery program led by licensed health professionals, and the first official NOX Pro Shop in the United States. Founding memberships are open now through Playtomic, starting at $225 per month or $2,250 per year for the first 100 members.</description_long>
<parameter name="lat">26.7095

StatusHistory.note: 26,000 sqft, 5 indoor padel courts at 2031 Okeechobee Blvd, opening 2026 (delivery fill)

Title: Soleste on the Trail
Description: A 255-unit, four-story garden-style apartment community on a 15.8-acre site along N Military Trail, anchored by a two-acre lake and resort amenities.
DescriptionLong: A 255-unit, four-story rental community rising on a 15.8-acre site at 7920 N Military Trail in Riviera Beach. One- to three-bedroom apartments range from 700 to 1,000 square feet, finished with stainless steel appliances, quartz countertops, and walk-in closets. The community is built around a two-acre lake with a walking trail and a slate of resort-style amenities including a swimming pool, fitness center, spa, and children's playground — minutes from the shops and restaurants at The Square. Developed by 7920 Riviera Beach Holdings, an affiliate of Miami-based Estate Companies, which acquired the site for $8 million in 2022.

StatusHistory.note: The Estate Companies secured a $60M construction loan from BB Americas Bank for the 255-unit rental

Title: The Avenue Naples
Description: A 50-unit ultra-luxury condominium and 75,000-sf retail and dining destination anchoring 5th Avenue South in downtown Naples.
DescriptionLong: The Avenue Naples brings 50 ultra-luxury condominiums above 75,000 square feet of retail and restaurants along the entrance to 5th Avenue South, Naples' historic main street. Residences feature private balconies and handcrafted finishes over a landmark retail and dining base, with 326 parking spaces split between valet and self-park. Amenities include a pool deck, concierge, and curated green spaces tied into the courtyards and streetscape of Old Naples.

StatusHistory.note: Four three-story buildings, 50 condos over ground-floor retail per Florida YIMBY/Gulfshore Business

Title: 15 CityPlace
Description: Designed by the acclaimed Arquitectonica, with ultra-luxurious interiors curated by Fogarty Finger — 15 CityPlace is crowned by a massive 6,000-square-foot rooftop sky garden, with its top five floors featuring sweeping, panoramic views of the Intracoastal Waterway and Palm Beach Island from private terraces. At the ground level, high-end retail boutiques and upscale, curated dining destinations will anchor The Square expansion.
DescriptionLong: Designed by the acclaimed Arquitectonica, with ultra-luxurious interiors curated by Fogarty Finger — 15 CityPlace is crowned by a massive 6,000-square-foot rooftop sky garden, with its top five floors featuring sweeping, panoramic views of the Intracoastal Waterway and Palm Beach Island from private terraces. At the ground level, high-end retail boutiques and upscale, curated dining destinations will anchor The Square expansion.

Title: The Berkeley
Description: A 25-story Arquitectonica-designed condominium overlooking Clear Lake, with 193 residences including four penthouses and interiors by founding principal Bernardo Fort-Brescia.
DescriptionLong: The Berkeley Palm Beach is a 25-story residential tower rising at 550 South Australian Avenue in downtown West Palm Beach's Clear Lake District, set just west of the Intracoastal and the CityPlace open-air complex. The building will introduce 193 luxury residences — including four penthouses — ranging from two to five bedrooms across roughly 2,500 to 4,800 square feet, with 9- to 11-foot ceilings, east- and west-facing flow-through layouts capturing sunrise and sunset exposures, oversized private terraces, Italian-crafted Snaidero kitchens, spa-style bathrooms, and integrated smart-home systems. Amenities are distributed across multiple levels: a 7th-floor family deck with pool, spa, fitness center, teen lounge, and children's play area, and a rooftop retreat with adult pool and jacuzzi, private dining, cigar lounge, nightclub, and panoramic views toward Palm Beach Island. Additional building features include wellness studios, a golf simulator, co-working spaces, indoor lounges, a gourmet resident market, full-time concierge and valet, UPLIN Health's in-home health services, and a building-wide emergency standby generator. Architecture is by Arquitectonica with interiors by founding principal Bernardo Fort-Brescia. The project is developed by Australian Property Group — led by Al Adelson, the developer behind The Bristol, the 2019 building widely credited with establishing West Palm Beach's modern luxury high-rise market — in partnership with Sympatico Real Estate, with exclusive sales by Douglas Elliman Development Marketing. Pricing runs from approximately $2 million to $10 million, with an average price of about $3.5 million, and the project has surpassed $100 million in pre-construction sales. Groundbreaking is anticipated for June 2026, with delivery targeted for 2028.

StatusHistory.note: 25-story per developer; groundbreaking June 2026, completion 2029 (May 2026 update) per PRWeb/Florida YIMBY

Title: Mr. C Residences West Palm Beach
Description: Hospitality‑focused hotel and residences, bringing the Mr. C brand’s European service to West Palm Beach for the first time.
DescriptionLong: Mr. C Hotel & Residences West Palm Beach broke ground in August 2025 as the first Mr. C property in Palm Beach County, rising in the heart of downtown West Palm Beach as a 27-story tower combining 110 hotel suites and 146 branded condominium residences. Designed by Arquitectonica with interiors by Meyer Davis, the building draws on the Italian Riviera of the 1950s and the city's sailing culture, with refined finishes, expansive layouts, and large windows framing views of the Intracoastal Waterway and downtown skyline; select residences include corner exposures and generous terraces. The amenity program centers on a garden-level lap pool with private cabanas and a poolside bar and lounge, alongside a fitness center with dedicated rooms for yoga, pilates, and Peloton workouts, and a spa with steam and sauna facilities. Residents also share a library, business lounge, children's play area, curated art installations, and a private members' lounge, all supported by a custom Mr. C Hotel & Residences digital app for building services and concierge requests. The project is developed by Miami-based Terra in partnership with Sympatico Real Estate, with exclusive sales by Douglas Elliman Florida; pre-sales had crossed 70 percent at groundbreaking. The Mr. C brand was founded by the Cipriani family in 2011 with its first hotel in Beverly Hills, later expanding to New York, Miami, and Dubai, and West Palm Beach extends that European-inspired hospitality program into Palm Beach County for the first time.

Title: Ziggurat Coconut Grove
Description: Conceptual mixed‑use project with terraced, gardened architecture envisioned for luxury residences and boutique retail/office.
DescriptionLong:
A sculptural mixed-use sanctuary in the heart of Coconut Grove, organized around 19 boutique residences, 100,000 square feet of Class-A office space, and 40,000 square feet of curated retail. The building's stepped, oolite-clad silhouette draws inspiration from ancient ziggurats and rises organically from the Grove's tree canopy — weaving native plantings, warm woods, and living greenery through every floor. Residences feature 11-foot ceilings, private elevators with double-door entry, 12-foot-deep terraces with full summer kitchens, European oak floors, travertine throughout, and chef's kitchens with Italian stone counters and a Wolf and Sub-Zero appliance package. Primary suites are finished with Dornbracht fixtures, deep soaking tubs, and dual-function rain showers, while integrated smart-home technology runs throughout. 

The rooftop is reserved as a private wellness sanctuary anchored by a hydrotherapy circuit, resort-style pool, private cabanas, a chef's grilling station, and a MICHELIN-starred restaurant with panoramic views of Biscayne Bay. At street level, a landscaped paseo threads couture ateliers, an artisanal café, a global gourmet market, and additional MICHELIN-starred dining — extending the wellness experience outward toward Kirk Munroe Park, set for a $6.3 million renovation directly across the street.

Title: Bone Valley at Streamsong

DescriptionLong: One of the world’s best golf destinations has officially unveiled its bold new fifth championship golf course, Bone Valley. Designed by acclaimed architect David McLay Kidd, known for his work at world-class destinations like Bandon Dunes — Bone Valley will bring another elite track to Streamsong, a property already home to three Top 40 public courses in the United States. With Bone Valley, Kidd joins an extraordinary roster of modern architectural icons at Streamsong - Coore & Crenshaw, Tom Doak, and Hanse & Wagner - forming the “Mount Rushmore” of modern minimalist golf designers. Preview play begins October 30, 2026, ahead of a January 26, 2027 opening, alongside new lakeside Golf Cabins designed for group stays.

Title: Delano Residences Miami
Description: The first-ever residential tower from the legendary Delano brand and marking PMG’s second supertall to rise over the city.
DescriptionLong:
A landmark 90-story supertall and the first-ever residential tower from the legendary Delano brand, marking PMG's second supertall to rise over Downtown Miami. The 421-residence project redefines urban luxury through a "vertical resort" model that integrates hotel-grade hospitality across two products: the Delano Collection for flexible short-term stays and Delano Residences for long-term living. 

The tower is anchored by South Florida's first public observation deck — featuring a cantilevered glass floor at 850 feet — alongside a sky pool perched at 800 feet and the legendary Rose Bar reimagined for a new skyline era. Residents tap into 24-hour concierge and valet, The Source by Delano wellness sanctuary, and private access to beach services at the original Delano Miami Beach.

StatusHistory.note: 90-story supertall, 421 residences per Robb Report / PROFILEmiami (sales launched 2026)

Title: Pier Sixty-Six
Description: A 32-acre Fort Lauderdale waterfront destination from Tavistock Development — anchored by a reopened 325-room resort, 88 private residences, a world-class marina, and a curated dining and retail collection — with a proposed Phase II expansion that would add four 23-story towers and 339 new residences.
DescriptionLong:
Pier Sixty-Six is a 32-acre waterfront mixed-use destination in Fort Lauderdale at 2301 SE 17th Street, anchored at the heart of the city's intracoastal corridor. The historic property's redevelopment concluded in January 2025 with the reopening of a 325-room resort, 88 private residences, a fully reimagined marina, and an integrated district of restaurants, retail, and public waterfront amenities. The Phase I redevelopment — designed by Arquitectonica and developed by Tavistock Development Company — restored the landmark observation tower at the project's center and introduced the resort's dining program, members-only spaces, and resort-level amenities across the campus.

In May 2026, Tavistock unveiled Phase II of the development: a proposed eight-acre mixed-use expansion that would add four 23-story towers rising 270 feet, distributing 339 additional residences across the four buildings. Building A, on a 1.4-acre north parcel, would deliver 101 residences; Buildings B, C, and D, on a 6.6-acre south parcel, would deliver 90, 75, and 73 residences respectively. The Phase II program adds ground-floor retail and restaurant space, second-level office space across all four towers, structured parking, and expanded marina facilities, alongside an approximately one-acre new water basin on the south side of the property that would extend marina access while increasing publicly accessible waterfront frontage.

The Phase II proposal also calls for additional grocery, retail, and dining space, mobility improvements, future public art installations throughout the property, and a new community gathering space beneath the 17th Street Causeway Bridge — planned in partnership with the City of Fort Lauderdale and the Florida Department of Transportation — that would activate an underutilized area beneath the bridge as a new public amenity linking both sides of the waterfront development.
ProjectType: Residences, Entertainment, Park, Retail, Hotel, Eateries, Marina

Title: Panther National
Description: Palm Beach County’s first new golf community in nearly two decades with a Nicklaus-Thomas course.
DescriptionLong:
A 400-acre private golf and luxury residential community, designed around an 18-hole championship golf course created by golf legends Jack Nicklaus and Justin Thomas. Located within the Avenir master-planned community and bordering more than 2,400 acres of protected conservation land, Panther National combines contemporary architecture, expansive homesites, and a club-focused lifestyle in one of South Florida’s fastest-growing luxury markets.

The community features approximately 218 custom and signature estate homes, many designed by acclaimed architect Max Strang, with modern architecture that stands apart from traditional South Florida golf communities. Amenities include a private golf club, lifestyle club, racquet facilities, wellness offerings, resort-style pools, and a new clubhouse currently under construction that is expected to become a centerpiece of the development.

Title: The Setai, St. Maarten
Description: An ultra-luxury beachfront resort and branded residences in Indigo Bay, marking The Setai's Caribbean debut
DescriptionLong: The Setai St. Maarten is an ultra-luxury beachfront resort and collection of branded residences in Indigo Bay, developed by Altree Developments in partnership with The Setai Hotels (Nakash Family). Set along over 1,000 feet of pristine shoreline with views toward Saba and St. Barths, the property will feature a 205-key hotel plus 280 private residences ranging from approximately 620 to over 2,000 sq ft, with dual-key options up to 6,000 sq ft. Designed by HKS Architects with interiors by Studio Munge, amenities include a beach club with cabanas and butler service, three pools (including an adults-only oasis), a 30,000 sq ft destination spa, tennis and pickleball courts, five restaurant concepts, and a Les Clefs d'Or concierge team. It will be St. Maarten's first Leading Hotels of the World property. Construction is underway with completion expected in 2028.

StatusHistory.note: 205 keys plus branded residences/villas; opening 2028 (unchanged) per Luxury Travel Magazine

Title: R&B Sports Center
Description: A private, invitation-only sports and wellness campus, combining elite tennis and multi-sport training with mentorship-focused programming, featuring courts, wellness facilities, and creative spaces.
DescriptionLong: A private, invitation-only sports and wellness campus in Wellington led by Betsy McCormack and Rodrigo Nascimento, combining elite tennis and multi-sport training with mentorship-focused programming, featuring courts, wellness facilities, and creative spaces, with design by Spina O'Rourke + Partners.

Title: Arbor Coconut Grove
Description: A boutique 45-residence, 5-story building in the heart of Coconut Grove, steps from CocoWalk
DescriptionLong: Arbor Coconut Grove is a boutique 5-story, 45-residence building developed by Muse Groupe in the Village of Coconut Grove, Miami. Designed by Miami-based Behar Font & Partners, the low-density building offers spacious 2-, 3-, and 4-bedroom layouts with flexible den spaces, floor-to-ceiling impact-resistant windows, and nature-inspired modernist design. Located at 3034 Oak Avenue, the building is steps from CocoWalk, parks, marinas, top-rated schools, restaurants, and shops. Amenities include a pool, rooftop, lobby, pet-friendly community spaces, electric car charging stations, and ample resident parking. Move-in ready Q1 2026.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Coconut Grove (per project name + official site)

Title: DELUX WeHo
Description: A 134-unit boutique wellness-focused apartment community in Wedgewood-Houston with saltwater pool, red light saunas, cold plunge, and Technogym fitness center.
DescriptionLong: A 134-unit boutique apartment community in Nashville's Wedgewood-Houston neighborhood, developed by The Bradley Projects. Following the same wellness-driven approach as the team's earlier project The Lanes, DELUX WeHo prioritizes natural light, livability, and intentional living over scale. Residences range from approximately 500 to 1,500 square feet across studio, one-, two-, and three-bedroom layouts, including loft-style floor plans that separate living and private spaces, with oversized windows and private balconies throughout. Wellness and recovery amenities include a saltwater pool, two red light saunas, a cold plunge, a dedicated recovery studio, and a Technogym fitness center. The property also features 5,000 square feet of street-level retail and sits within two blocks of Soho House Nashville, Michelin-starred Bastion, Hermès Nashville, and Pastis.
Developer: The Bradley Projects, Tinsley Co., Big Plan Holdings

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Wedgewood-Houston (WeHo), Nashville (per official site)

Title: Skyline OC
Description: Orange County's tallest residential building, a 25-story dual-tower condo conversion bringing resort-style ownership to South Coast Metro
DescriptionLong: Skyline OC is a 25-story, dual-tower condo conversion by Miami-based developer Crescent Heights, restoring ownership to Orange County's tallest residential building at 9 MacArthur Place in Santa Ana. Originally built in 2010 as condominiums and later operated as rentals, the towers have been fully reimagined with sophisticated interiors, hotel-caliber amenities, and an ownership-focused resident culture. Move-in-ready residences range from one- and two-bedroom homes to penthouses up to $3 million, with prices starting around $600,000. Average unit size is approximately 1,500 square feet — significantly larger than newly built condos in comparable markets — and homes feature floor-to-ceiling windows, open layouts, and chef-worthy kitchens with Viking, LG, and Miele appliances. The 60,000 square feet of amenity space includes an 80-foot pool with teak lounges and private cabanas, a fitness center with yoga and Pilates studios, spa treatment rooms, sauna and steam areas, a bocce court, putting green, co-working lounge, and chef's kitchen. Services include 24-hour security and an on-site concierge. Sales are led by Polaris Pacific. Located near John Wayne Airport, UC Irvine, and Newport Beach.

Title: DUOS Wynwood
Description: A new 49-unit, eight-story short-term rental hospitality concept rising in the heart of Miami's Wynwood Arts District
DescriptionLong: A new 49-unit, eight-story short-term rental hospitality concept in the heart of Miami's Wynwood Arts District. DUOS was purpose-designed from the ground up specifically for short-term rentals—allowing buyers to fully own their unit while renting it out for stays ranging from a single night up to 30 days. Architecture by MKDA and interiors by award-winning international hospitality design firm AvroKo create a non-branded, design-driven identity rooted in eclectic modernism. The 49-unit scale offers a curated, boutique alternative to Miami's larger 200-plus-unit developments, with studio to two-bedroom suites ranging from the $500,000s to over $1 million. Construction is expected to break ground in late 2026, with completion anticipated in early 2028.
Developer: Wave Group Development, Vitrium Capital, Zambala Luxury Apartments
